title: Rulebricks Chart Values
description: Configuration values for the Rulebricks umbrella chart.

domain.description: Base domain for the deployment (e.g., rb.example.com)

version.description: Rulebricks product version used by app, HPS, HPS worker, and migration images

imageRegistry.description: Single registry host override applied to every image (empty = docker.io/rulebricks/*). Replaces the host, keeps the rulebricks/<name> path.

imageDigests.description: Generated name->sha256 digest map (scripts/images/render-digests.sh); when a name is present the image helper pins @sha256 instead of :tag

images.description: Shared leaf images (init/hook/test utilities) as {registry, repository, tag} dicts rendered through the image helper

imagePullSecrets.description: Pull secrets for the private rulebricks/* images (and any custom registry the imageRegistry override points at)

externalDnsEnabled.description: Enable external-dns integration for automatic DNS record management

priorityClasses.description: Release-scoped scheduling priority tiers: <release>-critical (1000000) for stateful infrastructure and <release>-burst (-5) for the worker fleet, so critical pods always preempt burst workers.

storage.description: Shared object storage: one provider, one identity, and one bucket/container with per-purpose path prefixes

bucket.description: Single bucket (S3/GCS) or storage account (azure-blob) holding all Rulebricks data

container.description: Single blob container holding all path prefixes

paths.description: Per-purpose key prefixes within the single bucket/container

anonKey.description: Public anon key the browser sends to the API gateway. Embedded into the app ConfigMap at template time; must be non-empty unless anonKeySecretRef supplies it at runtime.

not.comment: The well-known Supabase self-hosting demo anon key (iss: supabase-demo, signed with the public demo secret). Shipped as a chart default before 2026-06-30; the gateway rejects it against real consumer keys, so carrying it forward from an old values file breaks every browser request.
The well-known Supabase self-hosting demo service_role key; see anonKey.

secretRef.description: Runtime alternative to an inline anonKey: an existing Secret carrying the public anon key, injected as an explicit env var on the app deployment (overrides the ConfigMap's template-time value). Same pattern as the supabase subchart's secret.* blocks; sources ONLY the anon key (other Supabase secrets ride global.secrets.secretRef).

secretRefKey.description: Override secret keys for the existing secret ref.

externalAuth.description: External Authentication / Token Passthrough for proxy-gated deployments

publicPaths.description: URL-path prefixes that bypass the token check

clickstack.description: Cross-chart switch for the built-in ClickStack observability backend.

tracing.description: Distributed tracing: in-cluster OpenTelemetry Collector that forwards OTLP spans from the app/HPS/workers/Traefik to a pluggable backend (Elastic APM, a generic OTLP/HTTP endpoint, or Azure Monitor).

image.description: RB image dict for the OpenTelemetry collector, rendered through the rulebricks.image helper (registry host overridable by global.imageRegistry).

elastic.description: Customer-managed Elastic APM target for OTLP spans (destination: elastic).

endpoint.description: OTLP endpoint of the Elastic APM server / managed integration
OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint of the backend

azureMonitor.description: Azure Monitor / Application Insights target (destination: azure-monitor).

allOf.comment: Default/absent destination is treated as elastic.

labels.description: Global labels applied to all resources

annotations.description: Global annotations applied to all resources

podLabels.description: Labels applied to all pod templates

podAnnotations.description: Annotations applied to all pod templates

scheduling.description: Opt-in scheduling defaults for Rulebricks workloads and migration jobs; dependency subcharts may require their own scheduling values

nodeSelector.description: Node selector applied to workloads that inherit global scheduling

tolerations.description: Tolerations applied to workloads that inherit global scheduling

affinity.description: Affinity rules applied to workloads that inherit global scheduling; prefer soft rules unless your platform requires hard placement

backup.description: Periodic self-hosted Supabase Postgres backups to shared object storage

superuser.description: Postgres superuser role used for logical dumps/restores so it can read schemas owned by supabase_admin (auth, storage, realtime, etc.)

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Time to keep completed backup jobs and their pod logs (seconds)

externalSecrets.description: Overlay-facing External Secrets metadata (not rendered directly by this chart)

remoteRefs.description: Map local keys to remote secret paths

metrics.description: Prometheus metrics scraping for the app and HPS

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for Rulebricks app pods

affinity.description: Affinity for Rulebricks app pods. Prefer soft rules unless your platform requires hard placement.

kafkaTopicPrefix.description: Prefix namespacing all Kafka topic names (e.g. com.rulebricks.). Empty string disables prefixing.

keda.description: Gather-plane autoscaler: scales HPS on response-topic lag. Conservative one-pod-at-a-time behavior because HPS scale events rebalance the response consumer group and can time out in-flight requests.

imagePrepull.description: DaemonSet that pre-pulls the HPS server + worker images onto every node so burst scale-outs skip the image pull on fresh nodes.

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for the prepull DaemonSet; empty by default so it reaches every node.

tolerations.description: Tolerations for the prepull DaemonSet; defaults to the Rulebricks burst-pool taint only. Add explicit architecture tolerations as needed; avoid bare {operator: Exists} because it also tolerates shutdown, out-of-service, and unreachable nodes.

resources.description: Per-container resources for the prepull DaemonSet (near-zero by default so it never affects bin-packing).

affinity.description: Affinity for HPS pods. Prefer soft rules unless your platform requires hard placement.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Optional custom topology spread constraints for HPS pods. Unset by default so HPS can prefer non-infrastructure nodes.

solutionPartitions.description: Partition count of the (prefixed) solution request topic. Must match kafka.provisioning.topics for the solution topic; hard ceiling on worker fleet concurrency, so keda.maxReplicaCount must not exceed it.

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClass for worker pods; defaults to the release-scoped burst class (<release>-burst) when global.priorityClasses.enabled, making workers the first preemption victims.

scalingBehavior.description: Optional HPA scaling behavior override (scaleUp/scaleDown) rendered into the worker ScaledObject; omit to use the chart's exponential scale-up / smooth scale-down defaults.

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for HPS worker pods

affinity.description: Affinity for HPS worker pods. Prefer soft rules unless your platform requires hard placement.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Optional custom topology spread constraints for HPS worker pods. Unset by default so workers can prefer non-infrastructure nodes.

redis.description: Valkey/Redis-compatible configuration. The redis key is retained for compatibility. Set enabled: false to use an external/managed endpoint.

external.description: External Valkey/Redis-compatible connection settings (used when redis.enabled is false).

password.description: Inline password. Prefer existingSecret for production.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing secret holding the Valkey/Redis-compatible password.

httpApi.description: Upstash-compatible HTTP API. When enabled, the in-cluster serverless-redis-http bridge is bypassed.

cache.description: Optional Valkey observability and management components.

valkeyAdmin.description: Official Valkey Admin web console. Disabled by default and internal-only unless explicitly exposed through authenticated ingress.

basicAuth.description: Traefik BasicAuth credentials. users must be htpasswd-formatted entries; prefer CLI generation.

redisExporter.description: Prometheus exporter for Valkey/Redis-compatible cache metrics.

kafkaExporter.description: Prometheus exporter for Kafka topic and consumer-group lag metrics.

serviceAccount.description: Dedicated ServiceAccount for the exporter; the IAM identity attachment point for MSK IAM (IRSA annotation or EKS Pod Identity association).

provisioning.description: Explicit topic management (create + idempotent alignment of existing topics via postScript). Topic names must carry the configured kafkaTopicPrefix.

kafkaBridge.description: Vector Kafka bridge sidecar for token-auth managed Kafka (AWS MSK IAM / GCP OAUTHBEARER) that Vector cannot speak natively.

persistence.description: Local MergeTree storage. Required while clickstack.enabled; set enabled: false (with ClickStack disabled) to run ClickHouse stateless over the object-storage decision-log archive - rendered as a Deployment instead of a StatefulSet, with no PVC.

clickstack.description: Built-in ClickStack observability backend (HyperDX UI, OTel collectors, FerretDB). The on/off switch is global.clickstack.enabled (the subchart's dependency condition, shared with sibling subcharts); the keys here only configure ClickStack when enabled.

ttl.description: Advanced ClickHouse exporter TTL override. Leave empty to derive from retentionDays.

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Time to keep completed jobs and their pod logs (seconds)

backoffLimit.description: Number of retries before marking job as failed

tolerations.description: Tolerations for migration job pods. Defaults are empty; add ARM64 tolerations only for clusters that taint ARM64 nodes.

kube-prometheus-stack.description: kube-prometheus-stack passthrough. Only remote_write Azure AD auth is validated here; the rest is owned by the subchart.

azureAd.description: Azure Monitor managed Prometheus auth. The active identity block must carry a non-empty client ID.

allOf.title: Managed Supabase Validation
allOf.description: If Supabase subchart is disabled (managed mode), URL and Access Token are required and must not be empty.

allOf.title: Self-hosted Supabase Validation
allOf.description: If Supabase subchart is enabled (self-hosted mode), JWT Secret is required and must not be empty.

allOf.title: Supabase Anon Key Validation
allOf.description: The browser client authenticates every API-gateway request with the public anon key, which the app ConfigMap embeds at TEMPLATE time - global.secrets.secretRef cannot supply it. Deliberately NOT excused by the app-level secretRef: either set global.supabase.anonKey inline (it is the public key, safe in values) or point global.supabase.secretRef at an existing Secret carrying it.

allOf.title: External Kafka Validation
allOf.description: If Kafka subchart is disabled, external brokers must be specified in rulebricks.app.logging.kafkaBrokers and must not be empty.

allOf.title: External Valkey/Redis-Compatible Validation
allOf.description: If in-cluster Valkey is disabled, an external Valkey/Redis-compatible host must be specified in rulebricks.redis.external.host and must not be empty.

allOf.title: External Database Validation
allOf.description: If supabase.externalDatabase is enabled (self-hosted Supabase services against an external PostgreSQL), the config must be workable end to end: (1) supabase.db.enabled must be false so the bundled Postgres, the in-cluster db alias, and the migration job all agree on the external server; (2) the host must be resolvable, either inline (externalDatabase.host) or from a Secret (externalDatabase.secretRef + secretRefKey.host); (3) the shared service-role password must have a source (supabase.secret.db.password inline, supabase.secret.db.secretRef, or externalDatabase.secretRef); (4) while externalDatabase.bootstrap.enabled is true (the default), master credentials are required (bootstrap.masterPassword inline or bootstrap.secretRef with keys master-username/master-password/service-password) - disable bootstrap only if you run bootstrap.sql yourself; (5) the migration job needs migrations.externalDb.host (it does not read externalDatabase.host).
Bundled Postgres must be explicitly disabled (supabase.db.enabled=false).
External host: externalDatabase.host inline, or externalDatabase.secretRef + secretRefKey.host.
Service-role credentials: supabase.secret.db.password inline, supabase.secret.db.secretRef, or externalDatabase.secretRef.
Bootstrap master credentials: masterPassword or bootstrap.secretRef, unless bootstrap.enabled=false.
Migration job host: migrations.externalDb.host (or the legacy supabase.auth.environment.DB_HOST seam).

allOf.title: AI Feature Validation
allOf.description: If AI is enabled, OpenAI API Key is required and must not be empty.

allOf.title: Inline Secrets Validation
allOf.description: If no external secretRef is provided (empty string), sensitive values must be provided inline.

allOf.title: External DNS Consistency
allOf.description: If external-dns subchart is enabled, global.externalDnsEnabled must also be true.

allOf.title: External Auth Validation
allOf.description: If externalAuth is enabled, a Supabase JWT secret is required (sourced from global.supabase.jwtSecret).

allOf.title: SSO Validation
allOf.description: If SSO is enabled, provider, clientId, and clientSecret are required.

allOf.title: Decision Log Query Storage Validation
allOf.description: Decision log query requires shared object storage to be configured.

allOf.title: Database Backup Storage Validation
allOf.description: Database backups require self-hosted Supabase and shared object storage to be configured.

allOf.title: Valkey Admin Ingress Auth Validation
allOf.description: Valkey Admin has no built-in authentication. If exposed through ingress, Traefik BasicAuth must be configured using htpasswd users or an existing Secret.
